tagline: Multi-agent orchestration for AI coding tools
subtitle: Thinking models think. Worker models execute. Everything runs in parallel with automatic review.

layers.name: Project Analysis
layers.summary: Cache tech stack and conventions in .hyperflow/

layers.name: Autonomy
layers.summary: Zero confirmations, minimal output, silent recovery

layers.name: Model Routing
layers.summary: Configurable thinking/worker per provider + priority chain

layers.name: Task Templates
layers.summary: Pre-built decomposition: CRUD, API, UI, migration, refactor, debug

skills.name: hyperflow
skills.purpose: Main orchestration that runs on every task

skills.name: init
skills.purpose: Analyze project, create .hyperflow/ cache, install multi-tool shims

skills.name: review
skills.purpose: L1 quick → L5 exhaustive review on changes, files, or PRs

skills.name: debug
skills.purpose: Systematic 5-Whys + hypothesis testing — never patches symptoms

skills.name: brainstorm
skills.purpose: Multi-dimensional analysis + alternatives — refuses to code before approval

skills.name: plan
skills.purpose: Decompose into parallel worker subtasks; writes task file; does not execute

skills.name: ship
skills.purpose: Lint, typecheck, build, tests, security sweep, commit, release, push

skills.name: memory
skills.purpose: Show, search, add, edit, prune, archive, clear, stats, migrate

capabilities: Parallel worker dispatch (independent subtasks run simultaneously)
Thinking-tier review of every worker output (iron rule)
Project-scoped persistent memory with tag taxonomy
Hot/warm/cold memory tiering with automatic compression
Lazy memory injection (only tag-matched entries per task)
Multi-tool auto-detection (AGENTS.md, Cursor rules, GEMINI.md, CLAUDE.md)
5 provider support (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Codex, Antigravity)
Auto-detect provider via env vars or folder presence
Security blocklists for sensitive files and destructive commands
Task tracking with incomplete-task recovery across sessions
